claim: Falsified artemisinin family drugs with no active ingredient are generally safe.

result.claim: Falsified artemisinin family drugs with no active ingredient are generally safe.
result.reasoning: The claim that falsified artemisinin family drugs with no active ingredient are generally safe is refuted by evidence from paper ID 2526777, "Manslaughter by Fake Artesunate in Asia—Will Africa Be Next?". This paper describes an epidemic of sophisticated counterfeit "artesunate" tablets containing no active ingredient, which have led to "fatal results" among the poorest and most vulnerable populations. This directly contradicts the idea that such drugs are generally safe.

evidence.text: The counterfeit “artesunate” tablets contain no artesunate.
evidence.source: Manslaughter by Fake Artesunate in Asia—Will Africa Be Next?

evidence.text: Amitriptyline is classified as a Group 1 drug.
evidence.source: Therapy of primary headaches: the role of antidepressants

evidence.text: Prostaglandin biosynthesis was inhibited by aspirin with an IC50 of 52 microM.
evidence.source: Prostaglandin biosynthesis in the rat lens.

evidence.text: Curli-DNA composites triggered production of autoantibodies in wild-type mice.
evidence.source: Amyloid-DNA Composites of Bacterial Biofilms Stimulate Autoimmunity.

evidence.text: Rapamycin is a highly specific and well-described drug approved for human use.
evidence.source: Mechanisms of Life Span Extension by Rapamycin in the Fruit Fly Drosophila melanogaster

evidence.text: Oligomycin, an ATP synthase inhibitor, did not mimic the effect of FCCP on CaWs.
evidence.source: Modulation of Intracellular Calcium Waves and Triggered Activities by Mitochondrial Ca Flux in Mouse Cardiomyocytes

evidence.text: Recent clinical trials support the safety of autologous MSC in SOT.
evidence.source: Mesenchymal stromal (stem) cells to improve solid organ transplant outcome: lessons from the initial clinical trials.

evidence.text: There were 59 safety communications for novel therapeutics.
evidence.source: Postmarket Safety Events Among Novel Therapeutics Approved by the US Food and Drug Administration Between 2001 and 2010

evidence.text: Endoxifen and N-desmethyl-tamoxifen exhibited non-competitive kinetics for both inhibitors.
evidence.source: Tamoxifen metabolites as active inhibitors of aromatase in the treatment of breast cancer

evidence.text: The 95% confidence interval (CI) for the proportion of novel therapeutics affected by a postmarket safety event at 10 years was 25.1%-37.5%.
evidence.source: Postmarket Safety Events Among Novel Therapeutics Approved by the US Food and Drug Administration Between 2001 and 2010
